methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Glyphic Biotechnologies is a Series A-stage biotech company founded by Joshua Yang and Daniel Estandian. The company has raised $44M to date. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Only one triangulation method yielded sufficient data for application. Stage-progression analysis, using the company's $44M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression, implies a valuation of $88M. Peer-median analysis proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow peer set (fewer than three datapoints met filtering criteria). Public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 6.9x across the three public tickers could not be applied without access to the target's revenue, which private companies rarely disclose.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $75M to $101M, with a median point of $88M. The primary constraint is methodological: reliance on a single applicable method limits triangulation robustness. Key uncertainties include Glyphic's unit economics, clinical development stage, and intellectual property strength relative to the named private peers—variables not captured in the current dataset but material to biotech valuation.
